What is the definition of a resultant vector?
correct_answer: The single vector which has the same effect as the original vectors acting together.
incorrect_answers: The sum of the magnitudes of the vectors.
A vector that acts in the opposite direction.

What is impulse?
correct_answer: Impulse is the product of the net force acting on an object and the time the force acts.
incorrect_answers: Impulse is the object's velocity divided by time.

Impulse is the force divided by acceleration.

Define momentum.
correct_answer: Momentum is the product of an object's mass and its velocity.
incorrect_answers: Momentum is the change in acceleration.
Momentum is speed multiplied by time.
Momentum is work divided by mass.

State Newton's First Law of Motion.
correct_answer: An object will remain at rest or continue to move at a constant velocity unless acted upon by a net external force.
incorrect_answers: For every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction.
The net force is equal to the rate of change of momentum.
The force of friction opposes motion.
An object will eventually stop unless a force is continuously applied.
